metadata.title: The Demo + Ship the 4 Canvas Files
metadata.playlist: Shop for Ingredients — Collect Your Madison Data
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 3 brief — Shop for Ingredients: Collect Your Madison Data

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 7 of 7. You have a clean data file, a tested workflow, and a documentation PDF. The last two things are the demo and the submission. The demo proves it runs. The submission is four files with exact filenames. Neither requires production skills — the grader wants to see the workflow execute and understand what you built. Today you pick your demo format, record or screenshot it, rename all four files, and ship them to Canvas. That is the whole job. Let's close it out.

props.segment: The Demo + Ship the 4 Canvas Files
props.command: Record the demo. Name the 4 files correctly. Submit to Canvas.
props.runningText: demo proves it runs — production quality does not matter…
output: Demo: 3-min video OR 5–8 captioned screenshot PDF
4 files: workflow JSON · documentation PDF · data file · demo

beats.narration_text: The demo proves the workflow runs. You have two options. Option A: a three-minute video. This does not need to be edited or polished. Open n8n, run the workflow on camera, and show the output data. The three things to show: the workflow overview — what the nodes are and how they connect; the workflow running — click Execute and show it succeeding; and the output data — open the saved file and show at least five rows. Three minutes. One take is fine. The grader is not evaluating your production skills. Option B: a five-to-eight screenshot PDF. Screenshots of the full workflow, key node configurations — the RSS URL, the API endpoint, the CSV path — the workflow running, and a sample of the output data. Each screenshot must be captioned with one sentence explaining what it shows. Add arrows to highlight the important parts. A screenshot PDF is faster to produce than a video and easier to annotate. Both options are equal in the grader's eyes. Pick the one you can finish in an hour.

artifactLines: Option A — 3-minute video: overview → run on camera → show output data. One take, no edits.
Show: workflow shape · Execute button click · success indicators · saved file with 5+ rows.
Option B — 5–8 screenshot PDF: full workflow · key node configs · running · sample output.
Each screenshot: one caption + arrows highlighting the important part.
Both options are equal. Pick the one you can finish in an hour.
props.sparkLine: Prove it runs. One take. No production required.

beats.narration_text: The Canvas submission is four files. Each has an exact filename format — use it exactly, because Canvas organizers and graders sort by filename. File one: the workflow JSON. Name it LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json. Before you submit, open it in a text editor one last time and search for your API key. If it appears, fix it now. File two: the documentation PDF. Name it LastName_FirstName_A3_Documentation.pdf. File three: the data file. Name it LastName_FirstName_A3_Data.csv or LastName_FirstName_A3_Data.json, depending on the format you chose. File four: the demo. If it is a video, name it LastName_FirstName_A3_Demo and keep the original file extension. If it is a screenshot PDF, name it LastName_FirstName_A3_Demo.pdf. Put all four in one folder. Look at them together before you upload anything. Count four files. Confirm the names match the format. Then upload.

artifactLines: 1. LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json — open and search for API key before submitting.
2. LastName_FirstName_A3_Documentation.pdf — verify quality numbers are the real ones.
3. LastName_FirstName_A3_Data.csv (or .json) — the saved output from the workflow.
4. LastName_FirstName_A3_Demo (video or .pdf) — the demo recording or screenshot PDF.
Check: 4 files in one folder. Names match the format. No real API keys visible.
props.sparkLine: Count 4. Names match. No real keys. Upload.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for The Demo and Submit. Demo format: three-minute video or five-to-eight screenshot PDF — both equal. Show the workflow shape, the workflow running, and at least five rows of output data. Four Canvas files, exact filenames: LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json, LastName_FirstName_A3_Documentation.pdf, LastName_FirstName_A3_Data.csv or .json, and LastName_FirstName_A3_Demo with whatever extension. Before uploading anything: open the workflow JSON and search for API keys. Count four files. Confirm the names match. The grader wants to see it run and understand what you built. Production quality does not matter. Clarity matters. A workflow that runs on two sources because one was unavailable is a better submission than a workflow that crashes gracefully on three. Ship it.

props.artifactTitle: The Demo + Ship the 4 Canvas Files
artifactLines: Demo: 3-min video OR 5–8 screenshot PDF. Show: shape · run · output data.
Pre-submit check: workflow JSON has no real API keys · 4 files · names match.
Clarity over production. A working 2-source workflow beats a crashed 3-source demo.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Before you record the real demo, do a dry run. Paste this prompt into Claude — then do what it says. The prompt reads: I am about to record a three-minute demo of my n8n workflow for Assignment 3. The workflow has these three sources: [list your three source nodes]. I will run the workflow start to finish on camera. List the five things most likely to go wrong during a live demo run — a node failing, a credential error, a missing file path, a slow API response, or something else specific to my sources — and tell me how to verify each one is working before I hit record. That prompt is a pre-flight checklist for your demo. Run through every item it gives you. Fix anything that is broken. Then run the workflow one final time, watch it succeed, and record that run. The thing that surprises you in the dry run is the thing that would have crashed the real demo.

props.segment: The Demo + Ship the 4 Canvas Files
props.command: I'm about to record a 3-min n8n demo for A3. My 3 sources: [list them]. List the 5 things most likely to fail during a live demo run — credential error, missing path, slow API, etc. — and tell me how to verify each one before I hit record.
props.runningText: run the dry-run checklist first — what surprises you is what would've crashed the real demo…

beats.narration_text: The Demo + Ship the 4 Canvas Files. Liam, in for Bear.
